title: hemerocallis fulva var. littorea
intro: Hemerocallis fulva var. littorea, commonly known as the Orange Daylily or Ditch Lily, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Hemerocallis genus. The plant is native to eastern Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Hemerocallis fulva var. littorea include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory and skin conditions, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and roots are typically dried, then used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, or crushed to create poultices for topical application.
